context: Kwaito — a genre blending house, hip-hop, and township rhythms — dominated South African music around 2000. Mandoza's 'Nkalakatha' became the first kwaito crossover hit to unite black and white audiences. Post-apartheid optimism and a vibrant youth culture fueled the genre. Artists like Arthur Mafokate, TKZee, and Bongo Maffin were household names.
